checks.leftCheek: The left cheek should be a white triangle in the right place.
checks.rightCheek: The right cheek should be a white triangle in the right place.
checks.leftEar: The left ear should be a brown triangle in the right place.
checks.rightEar: The right ear should be a brown triangle in the right place.
checks.leftFace: The left face should be an orange triangle in the right place.
checks.rightFace: The right face should be an orange triangle in the right place.
checks.nose: The nose needs two charcoal triangles. You can split it top/bottom or left/right.
checks.leftFaceAboveEar: The left face should be drawn **on top of** the left ear.
checks.rightFaceAboveEar: The right face should be drawn **on top of** the right ear.
checks.noseAboveFace: The nose should sit **on top of** the face.

drawFox.name: Draw the fox face
drawFox.description: Use triangles to build a geometric fox face on the grey background.

drawFox.name: Draw the fox face
drawFox.description: Build a geometric fox face using triangles.

callTriangle.question: How do I call the `triangle` function?
callTriangle.answer: Pass 7 arguments in order: the three corner points (`x1, y1, x2, y2, x3, y3`) and then the color string, e.g. `triangle(50, 10, 30, 50, 70, 50, "orange")`.

drawOrder.question: What order should I draw the parts in so they overlap correctly?
drawOrder.answer: Later shapes sit on top of earlier ones. Draw the white cheeks first so the orange face halves cover most of them, leaving just the cheek tips showing.

colors.question: Which colors go where?
colors.answer: The ears are `"brown"`, the face halves are `"orange"`, the cheeks are `"white"`, and the nose is `"charcoal"`. Remember the colors are strings, so they need quotes.

symmetry.question: How do I work out the coordinates on the right side without hovering?
symmetry.answer: The fox is symmetrical around the vertical middle. If a left-side point is at x=`30` and the centre is at x=`50`, the mirror point is at x=`70` (the same distance the other side of the centre). The y values stay the same.

multiplesOf5.question: Why aren't my coordinates working?
multiplesOf5.answer: All the values you need are multiples of 5. If a corner looks slightly off, you have probably used a number like `12` or `38`. Snap it to the nearest multiple of 5 (`10`, `15`, `40`, etc).

triangle.description: Draw a triangle with three corner points and a color
